overview: Creates a translation package (kit) from a set of input documents processed through Okapi filter events. Supports multiple output formats including Generic XLIFF, PO, OmegaT, XLIFF 2.0, and others. The extracted files can be merged back after translation using the **Rainbow Translation Kit Merging Step**. Typically used after a Raw Document to Filter Events step, optionally preceded by segmentation and leveraging steps.

writerClass.help:
Fully-qualified Java class name that determines the **package format** (kit type) to create. Each writer class produces a different translation kit format.

Available writers:
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.xliff.XLIFFPackageWriter` — Generic XLIFF
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.po.POPackageWriter` — PO Package
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.rtf.OriginalRTFPackageWriter` — Original with RTF layer
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.rtf.XLIFFRTFPackageWriter` — XLIFF with RTF layer
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.omegat.OmegaTPackageWriter` — OmegaT Project
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.transifex.TransifexPackageWriter` — Transifex Project
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.ontram.OntramPackageWriter` — ONTRAM XINI
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.table.TablePackageWriter` — Translation Table (tab-delimited)
- `net.sf.okapi.steps.rainbowkit.xliff.XLIFF2PackageWriter` — XLIFF 2.0
writerClass.values: Fully-qualified Java class name of a Rainbow kit package writer implementation. Must be on the classpath.
notes: Not all package types have additional options. Some formats (like Transifex) upload to external services rather than producing local files.
The package format affects whether `sendOutput` has any effect — formats that do not produce one output file per input file ignore that option.

packageName.help: Name of the package directory that will be created inside the output directory. This becomes the top-level folder containing the extracted translation files, manifest, and support materials.

packageDirectory.help:
Root directory where the translation package will be created. The actual package is placed in a subdirectory named by **packageName** within this directory.

Supports pipeline variables:
- `${inputRootDir}` — root directory of the input files
- `${rootDir}` — pipeline root directory
- Source/target locale variables: `${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.
packageDirectory.values: Directory path. Supports `${inputRootDir}`, `${rootDir}`, and locale variables (`${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.).

supportFiles.help:
List of additional files to include in the translation package as support material. Each item specifies an **origin** path and a **destination** within the package.

**Origin** paths support `*` and `?` wildcards, plus variables (`${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, locale variables).

**Destination** paths must start with `/` or `\` (relative to the package root). Use `<same>` to keep the original filename. When origin does not use wildcards, the destination can rename the file.
supportFiles.values: Semicolon-separated list of origin→destination file mapping pairs. Origin supports wildcards (`*`, `?`) and variables.
examples: Destination: `/resources/<same>` — places files in a `resources` subfolder keeping original names

message.help: Optional message or note to include with the translation package. Can be used to provide instructions or context to translators.

outputManifest.help: Controls whether a manifest file is generated with the package. The manifest is required by the **Rainbow Translation Kit Merging Step** to merge translated files back into the original format.
notes: Disabling this will prevent the package from being mergeable using the standard merging step.

createZip.help: Compresses the generated package into a single ZIP file with a `.rkp` (Rainbow Kit Package) extension. The ZIP file is placed in the same directory as the package and shares its name.
notes: The `.rkp` extension is specific to Okapi Rainbow translation kits.

sendOutput.help:
Instead of passing the original filter events downstream, sends the **prepared/extracted files** as raw documents to the next pipeline step. This allows further processing of the kit output files.

Only effective when the selected package format produces one output file per input file — formats that bundle multiple inputs into a single output ignore this option.
notes: Has no effect if the selected package format does not support one output file for each input file.

dependsOn.condition: only effective when the selected writer produces one output file per input file

limitations: If the pipeline modifies input files before this step (e.g., via Search and Replace), the "original" files saved in the package may not match the extracted content, making merge-back impossible without manual correction.
Inline code count changes from upstream steps (e.g., search and replace adding/removing codes) will cause merge failures — the saved originals must reflect the content at the point of extraction.
processingNotes: Takes **filter events** as input (requires a Raw Document to Filter Events step upstream).
Sends back either **filter events** (original) or **raw documents** (extracted files), depending on the `sendOutput` setting.
Requires both source and target language to be set in the pipeline.
This is a **batch-oriented** step — it accumulates documents and produces a complete translation kit.

examples.title: Simple extraction pipeline
examples.description: Minimal pipeline that extracts documents into a Generic XLIFF translation kit.
examples.config:
```yaml
steps:
  - Raw Document to Filter Events
  - Rainbow Translation Kit Creation
```

examples.title: Full pre-translation pipeline
examples.description: Segments text and leverages existing translations from two TMs before creating the kit. This is one of the pre-defined pipelines available in Rainbow.
examples.config:
```yaml
steps:
  - Raw Document to Filter Events
  - Segmentation
  - Leveraging (TM 1)
  - Leveraging (TM 2)
  - Rainbow Translation Kit Creation
```
